what_it_does: Tells Git to ignore all files ending in .service and .timer inside this generated directory.
observations: Contains only two wildcard patterns: *.service and *.timer
Patterns match systemd unit file extensions
Located under a directory named generated/
No comments, negations, or directory-specific rules
standouts: Ignores generated systemd service and timer unit files
Implies these artifacts are build outputs, not source
notes: A minimal gitignore that keeps generated systemd .service and .timer files out of version control.
questions: Which tool or process generates these .service and .timer files?
Should other generated systemd unit types (e.g., .socket, .target, .mount) also be ignored?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (no stated purpose in the file; behavior aligns with the generated/ path)
apparent_themes: Exclude generated artifacts from version control
Keep source definitions separate from build outputs
intention_signals: Prevent automatically generated systemd unit files from being tracked by Git.
contribution: Records the specific generated file extensions (.service, .timer) that should be excluded from version control in this directory, helping maintain the source-vs-generated boundary.
summary_for_embedding: A .gitignore file in company/ops/systemd/generated/ that excludes all .service and .timer files from Git tracking.
intention_for_embedding: To keep generated systemd service and timer unit files out of version control.
